Drafted0 / 110
Review-ready0 / 110
Reviewed0 / 110
Objective
Actions
Appearance
Import into this working copy

Word comments and tracked edits are imported as proposed review items. Nothing is applied automatically.

Workspace mode

Single-System remains the default. Portfolio mode is local-only and optional.

Review and delivery
Recovery
Danger zone
Download and export
Download / export
Word review round tripTrack Changes is enabled in the exported DOCX. Upload the reviewed copy through Import Center to reconcile feedback.
Optional workspace architecture

SSP portfolio workspace

Single-System remains the default. Portfolio mode adds governed module applicability and responsibility without rewriting the anchor SSP.

Current modeSingle-System

One SSP workspace. Existing JSON, CRM CSV, Word Review, and Workshop exchanges remain unchanged.

Release boundary

v1.9.5 adds a deterministic portfolio role coverage and responsibility matrix with portable exports and no identity authentication, access enforcement, personnel validation, findings, scoring, or certification conclusion.

Continue with one SSP—or prepare a portfolio

No change is required. Existing and new workspaces continue in Single-System mode. Conversion preserves all SSP fields, statuses, tables, images, review state, and L2G provenance.

Migration preview

Requirements110
Populated fields0
Status records0
Tables0
Before conversion: the tool creates a complete local rollback snapshot. Conversion adds one anchor module and 110 pending-decision records around the existing SSP; it does not infer applicability, implementation, responsibility, or approvals.

Portfolio record

The anchor module represents the current SSP. Each module has 110 explicit applicability and responsibility decision records; undecided values remain visible gaps.

Exchange import preview

Existing governed values are never replaced automatically. Cross-portfolio and cross-requirement identities fail closed.

Portfolio change calendar

Derive review-cycle dates and documented change milestones from the local portfolio record.

Not evaluated
Planning context only. Dates are derived from locally recorded review cycles, evidence dates, actions, reviews, approvals, baselines, and register entries. This does not create remote calendar events, send notifications, impose regulatory deadlines, or determine assessment, readiness, risk, compliance, or certification.
0Events0Overdue0Due soon0Upcoming0Historical0Unscheduled
Enable portfolio mode to calculate the change calendar.

Portfolio role coverage and responsibility matrix

Derive administrative responsibility coverage from local owner, review-role, evidence, action, inheritance, and review-cycle records.

Not evaluated
Administrative coverage only. Unassigned, multiply assigned, inherited, and unresolved rows are follow-up indicators—not assessment findings or compliance gaps. Names and identifiers are local labels, not authenticated identities. This view does not synchronize directories, enforce access, validate personnel, score readiness or risk, or make certification conclusions.
0Rows0Assigned0Unassigned0Multiple0Inherited0Unresolved0Not applicable
Enable portfolio mode to calculate responsibility coverage.

Module dependency map

Derive parent hierarchy and requirement-inheritance relationships from the local portfolio record.

Not evaluated
Documented relationships only. This view uses locally authored module and requirement records. It does not discover network paths, validate architecture, determine segmentation effectiveness, or produce assessment, readiness, risk, compliance, or certification conclusions.
0Modules0Relationships0Inherited requirements0Parent links0Shared-service links0Reference / cycle issues
Enable portfolio mode to calculate the dependency map.
No dependency relationships are available.

Evidence freshness and ownership reminders

Derive local reminders from evidence references, ownership metadata, validation dates, and review-cycle policy.

Not evaluated
Local reminder view only. The tool recalculates reminders on demand from locally stored data. It does not send email, create calendar events, run background monitoring, assess evidence sufficiency, determine control effectiveness, or produce readiness/compliance conclusions.
0Reminder records0Current0Due soon0Stale / expired0Owner follow-ups0Unresolved references
Enable portfolio mode to calculate evidence reminders.

Portfolio health and maintenance dashboard

Review deterministic administrative and authoring follow-up items across the portfolio. No readiness or compliance score is calculated.

Not evaluated
Operational authoring view only. The dashboard identifies missing or unresolved local records such as ownership references, applicability decisions, review state, inheritance follow-up, conflicts, and baseline administration. It does not determine implementation effectiveness, assessment status, readiness, certification, or compliance.
0Queue records0Indicators0Requirements in scope0Open conflicts0Governance follow-ups
Enable portfolio mode to calculate the local maintenance queue.

Portfolio reporting and delivery package

Build a recipient-ready package with deterministic operational summaries and a SHA-256 file inventory.

Not built
Verify before use. Delivery packages are local authoring and operational artifacts. Every embedded file is inventoried with SHA-256 and the package fails closed when content, paths, identities, or manifest fingerprints do not match. Summaries do not provide an assessment score, readiness rating, certification decision, or compliance conclusion.

Build a delivery package

0Files0BytesDraftReviewNoneBaseline

Verify package and extracted files

First verify the downloaded package JSON. To verify an extracted folder, use the included Python verifier or select the extracted directory below.

No delivery package has been verified.

Recipient handling instructions and the verification utility are included in each package. Local role labels and recipient labels are not authenticated identities.

Inheritance and reconciliation policy profiles

Safe-assisted policy is active by default.

Built-in default
Governed behavior, fixed identities. Profiles may control whether eligible inherited content and non-conflicting reconciliation candidates are applied after an explicit preview or retained for review. Profiles cannot permit cross-portfolio identities, fingerprint mismatches, protected identifier changes, conflict overwrites, or silent replacement of governed values. These profiles are not digital signatures or authenticated identity.

Select and activate a profile

Uses the current actor from Formal Review. Activation requires an active Approver or Administrator assignment for the selected scope and a rationale.

Create an immutable custom profile

Creation requires a portfolio Administrator. Profiles are append-only; create a new version instead of editing or deleting an existing record.

Available profiles

Activation history

Formal review and approval workflow

No formal review submission is active.

Draft
Local governance, not authentication or assessment. Names and roles are locally asserted. Approval records are tied to an exact content fingerprint and become stale when governed content changes. They do not determine CMMC compliance, readiness, certification, or assessment results.

Current actor and scope

Role assignments

Submit for review

Reviewer disposition

Approval or revocation

Immutable named baselines

A fresh formal approval is required before creating a baseline.

Needs approval
Immutable local snapshot, not a digital signature. A baseline captures the exact approved authoring scope and deterministic fingerprint. Existing baseline records are never edited or deleted. A later baseline supersedes through an append-only event, and restoration creates a new working version.

Create named baseline

Compare baselines

Choose two baselines.

Restore through a new version

Administrator role is required. A full JSON backup is offered before the current working scope is replaced.

Consolidated change and decision register

No portfolio governance events are available.

0 entries
Derived traceability, not a replacement source of truth. This register consolidates existing source records without rewriting them. Explicit notes are append-only. The register does not create authentication, digital signatures, assessment findings, certification decisions, or compliance conclusions.

Record an explicit governance note

Uses the current actor in the formal review section. The selected role must be actively assigned for the chosen scope.

Register filters

0Total0Decisions0Changes0Open / active0Approvals / baselines

Register entry

Parent-change impact review

No stale inherited records require review.

0

Parent change

Pending

Explicit decisions only. Refresh accepts current parent values and clears a local override. Preserve keeps the child snapshot, defer leaves it queued, and escalate creates an open conflict record. Local supplements remain separate in every path.

Portfolio CRM workbench

Derived responsibility rows preserve module detail and count inherited implementation only at its authoritative source.

0
Derived, not duplicated. Inherited base implementation and evidence remain reference-only on child rows. Local supplements and overrides stay visible and independently attributable. CRM output does not create an assessment, approval, certification, or readiness conclusion.
Filtered rows0
Authoritative implementations0
Inherited references0
Pending records0
Open conflicts0
Requirement / moduleClassification / sourceResponsibility / evidenceReview / conflict

CRM reconciliation preview

Module hierarchy

Stable IDs and parent-child integrity are enforced.

Add module

Module applicability and responsibility

Select a module to review all 110 requirements.

v1.8.4 CRM model
Total records110
Pending decisions110
Applicable0
Stale inheritance0
Local overrides0

Requirement

Deterministic authoring support, not a compliance conclusion. Inherited fields are copied from an eligible same-requirement source and fingerprinted. Parent changes mark the child stale; they never overwrite local work silently.

Local / not inherited

Eligible sources are an ancestor or an active shared-service module. Saving resolves the same requirement deterministically and records a canonical fingerprint.
Local supplement: inherited base fields remain source-derived. Record only the residual implementation and references owned by this module.
A local override preserves editable local fields instead of applying source values. A rationale is mandatory and the source fingerprint is still recorded for stale detection.
Known v1.8.8 boundary: the consolidated register is a local authoring-governance view and export, not an authenticated audit service, digital signature, assessment finding, certification decision, or legal custody record. Source records remain authoritative and are never rewritten by the register.
Document control

Create document revision

Create a controlled SSP revision and add the entry to Record of Changes.

Word review round trip

Imported Word review queue

Document navigation

Use families to move through the SSP without a second full-length control list.

Authoring guidance is visible. Guidance, instructions, helper callouts, workspace controls, and release notes are automatically excluded from print/PDF output.
{CLIENT_LOGO}

System Security Plan

Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Level 2
{CLIENT_NAME} {SYSTEM_NAME}
{DOCUMENT_VERSION}
{DOCUMENT_DATE}
This refined version keeps the SSP purpose intact while improving readability, editability, print fidelity, and placeholder consistency for browser and PDF workflows.
Purpose retainedSystem identification, environment, and all 110 Level 2 requirements remain structured for completion and review.
AI-friendly placeholdersEditable fields use curly-brace tokens so drafting, find/replace, and downstream automation are easier.
Ready to printThe page stays browser-editable but also prints cleanly to PDF without losing the document rhythm.

Document Control and Contents

Use this template to document the system boundary, environment of operation, implementation approach for each CMMC Level 2 requirement, and the supporting evidence needed for review.

Company-fit fields. Structured CSP inheritance and ESP shared-responsibility fields support firm-standard drafting patterns. Final disclosure, handling, retention, and CUI-marking language must still be approved through the appropriate QMS/Legal/client process.
Operational file handling. Populated HTML working copies, JSON backups, internal-review copies, CRM exports, Word files, and PDFs may contain client-sensitive or CUI-bearing information. Store them only in approved project/client locations, avoid unmanaged Downloads/Desktop copies, and purge temporary copies under the applicable engagement, records, and CUI-handling procedures.
Purpose retained. This refinement keeps the original SSP intent and retained requirement content, while improving hierarchy, completion flow, reviewer usability, and PDF-ready presentation.
{DOCUMENT_OWNER}
{APPROVAL_AUTHORITY}
{SSP_REVIEW_FREQUENCY}
{DISTRIBUTION_AND_HANDLING}

Review Workflow Guide

Review status applies to the current SSP document revision. Reviewers use the status, notes, and author-response fields inside each requirement.

Not Reviewed
Default / awaiting review

No assigned SME or Quality Reviewer has completed review for this document revision.

Needs Revision
Open reviewer action

Use when an SME or Quality Reviewer requires clarification, evidence, correction, or an author response.

Reviewed
Reviewer work complete

The assigned reviewer completed review and no reviewer action remains, but final approval is still pending.

Approved
Authorized approval

Use only when the authorized approver accepts the requirement for the current document revision.

Revision rule: creating a new document revision can reset Reviewed and Approved controls to Not Reviewed so the new revision receives a fresh review.

Record of Changes

DateDescription of changesMade byVersionApproval / review reference
{CHANGE_DATE}{CHANGE_DESCRIPTION}{CHANGED_BY}{DOCUMENT_VERSION}{APPROVAL_REFERENCE}

Completion guidance: Describe the system mission, business purpose, supported services, primary users, and why the system processes, stores, or transmits CUI.

Population / roleApproximate end usersApproximate privileged usersNotes

CUI category / information typeSourceRecipients / destinationsProcessing, storage, or transmissionHandling / dissemination controls

2 · System Environment

Completion guidance: Describe the assessment boundary, system interconnections, key devices and services, CUI flows, security protection assets, external service providers, remote access paths, and trust boundaries. Insert or reference the current system boundary diagram.
{SYSTEM_BOUNDARY_TOPOLOGY_NARRATIVE}
{SYSTEM_BOUNDARY_DIAGRAM}

2.1 · System Component Inventory

Completion guidance: Include or reference an authoritative inventory of hardware, virtual assets, network devices, appliances, specialized assets, and responsible owners.
{SYSTEM_COMPONENT_INVENTORY_REFERENCE}

2.2 · Software Component Inventory

Completion guidance: Include or reference an authoritative software, firmware, application, platform, and service inventory with versions and ownership.
{SOFTWARE_COMPONENT_INVENTORY_REFERENCE}

2.3 · Hardware and Software Maintenance and Ownership

Completion guidance: Identify owned, leased, managed, hosted, and externally maintained components. Document external service providers and shared-responsibility boundaries.
{MAINTENANCE_AND_OWNERSHIP_MODEL}

3 · Requirements

For each security requirement, document the implementation approach, ownership, supporting evidence, any inherited/shared responsibility, and the rationale for planned or non-applicable status.

Completion aid: Status and implementation statement preserve the source template. Responsibility, evidence, inheritance, and scope-note fields are added to improve traceability and review without changing the security requirement.
3.1

Access Control (AC)

22 security requirements

3.1.1

Authorized Access Control

Not startedReview prompts

Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems).

Assessment objectives 6
  1. Authorized users are identified.
  2. Processes acting on behalf of authorized users are identified.
  3. Devices (and other systems) authorized to connect to the system are identified.
  4. System access is limited to authorized users.
  5. System access is limited to processes acting on behalf of authorized users.
  6. System access is limited to authorized devices (including other systems).
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_1_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_1_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_1_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_1_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_1_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_1_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_1_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_1}
3.1.2

Transaction & Function Control

Not startedReview prompts

Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. The types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute are defined.
  2. System access is limited to the defined types of transactions and functions for authorized users.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_1_2}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_1_2}

{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_2}
3.1.3

Control CUI Flow

Not startedReview prompts

Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations.

Assessment objectives 5
  1. Information flow control policies are defined.
  2. Methods and enforcement mechanisms for controlling the flow of CUI are defined.
  3. Designated sources and destinations (e.g., networks, individuals, and devices) for CUI within the system and between interconnected systems are identified.
  4. Authorizations for controlling the flow of CUI are defined.
  5. Approved authorizations for controlling the flow of CUI are enforced.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_3}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_3}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_3}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_1_3}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_1_3}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.

{ESP_PROVIDER_3_1_3}

{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_3}
3.1.4

Separation of Duties

Not startedReview prompts

Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. The duties of individuals requiring separation are defined.
  2. Responsibilities for duties that require separation are assigned to separate individuals.
  3. Access privileges that enable individuals to exercise the duties that require separation are granted to separate individuals.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_4}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_4}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_4}

3.1.5

Least Privilege

Not startedReview prompts

Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Privileged accounts are identified.
  2. Access to privileged accounts is authorized in accordance with the principle of least privilege.
  3. Security functions are identified.
  4. Access to security functions is authorized in accordance with the principle of least privilege.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_5}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_5}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_5}

3.1.6

Non-Privileged Account Use

Not startedReview prompts

Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing nonsecurity functions.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Non-security functions are identified.
  2. Users are required to use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions.
Implementation status

3.1.7

Privileged Functions

Not startedReview prompts

Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Privileged functions are defined.
  2. Non-privileged users are defined.
  3. Non-privileged users are prevented from executing privileged functions.
  4. The execution of privileged functions is captured in audit logs.
Implementation status

3.1.8

Unsuccessful Logon Attempts

Not startedReview prompts

Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. The means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is defined.
  2. The defined means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is implemented.

For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_1_8}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_8}
3.1.9

Privacy & Security Notices

Not startedReview prompts

Provide privacy and security notices consistent with applicable CUI rules.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Privacy and security notices required by CUI-specified rules are identified, consistent, and associated with the specific CUI category.
  2. Privacy and security notices are displayed.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_9}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_9}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_9}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_1_9}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_1_9}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_1_9}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_1_9}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_1_9}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_1_9}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_1_9}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_9}
3.1.10

Session Lock

Not startedReview prompts

Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays to prevent access and viewing of data after a period of inactivity.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. The period of inactivity after which the system initiates a session lock is defined.
  2. Access to the system and viewing of data is prevented by initiating a session lock after the defined period of inactivity.
  3. Previously visible information is concealed via a pattern-hiding display after the defined period of inactivity.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_10}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_10}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_10}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_1_10}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_1_10}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_1_10}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_1_10}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_1_10}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_1_10}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_1_10}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_10}
3.1.11

Session Termination

Not startedReview prompts

Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Conditions requiring a user session to terminate are defined.
  2. A user session is automatically terminated after any of the defined conditions occur.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_11}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_11}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_11}

Control Remote Access

Not startedReview prompts

Monitor and control remote access sessions.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Remote access sessions are permitted.
  2. The types of permitted remote access are identified.
  3. Remote access sessions are controlled.
  4. Remote access sessions are monitored.

Remote Access Confidentiality

Not startedReview prompts

Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions are identified.
  2. Cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions are implemented.

  2. Remote access is routed through managed network access control points.

Privileged Remote Access

  4. Access to the identified security-relevant information via remote access is authorized.

Encrypt CUI On Mobile

Not startedReview prompts

Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

  2. Encryption is employed to protect CUI on identified mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

External Connections

Not startedReview prompts

Verify and control/limit connections to and use of external systems.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. Connections to external systems are identified.
  2. The use of external systems is identified.
  3. Connections to external systems are verified.
  4. The use of external systems is verified.
  5. Connections to external systems are controlled/limited.
  6. The use of external systems is controlled/limited.

Portable Storage Use

Not startedReview prompts

Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. The use of portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is identified and documented.
  2. Limits on the use of portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems are defined.
  3. The use of portable storage devices containing CUI on external systems is limited as defined.

3.1.22

Control Public Information

Not startedReview prompts

Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.

Assessment objectives 5
  1. Individuals authorized to post or process information on publicly accessible systems are identified.
  2. Procedures to ensure CUI is not posted or processed on publicly accessible systems are identified.
  3. A review process is in place prior to posting of any content to publicly accessible systems.
  4. Content on publicly accessible systems is reviewed to ensure that it does not include CUI.
  5. Mechanisms are in place to remove and address improper posting of CUI.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_1_22}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_1_22}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_1_22}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_1_22}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_1_22}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_1_22}
3.2

Awareness and Training (AT)

3 security requirements

3.2.1

Role-Based Risk Awareness

Not startedReview prompts

Ensure that managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of those systems.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Security risks associated with organizational activities involving CUI are identified.
  2. Policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of the system are identified.
  3. Managers, systems administrators, and users of the system are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities.
  4. Managers, systems administrators, and users of the system are made aware of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of the system.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_2_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_2_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_2_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_2_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_2_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_2_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_2_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_2_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_2_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_2_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_2_1}
3.2.2

Role-Based Training

Not startedReview prompts

Ensure that personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties and responsibilities.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities are defined.
  2. Information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities are assigned to designated personnel.
  3. Personnel are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_2_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_2_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_2_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_2_2}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_2_2}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_2_2}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_2_2}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_2_2}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_2_2}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_2_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_2_2}
3.2.3

Insider Threat Awareness

Not startedReview prompts

Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Potential indicators associated with insider threats are identified.
  2. Security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat is provided to managers and employees.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_2_3}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_2_3}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_2_3}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_2_3}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_2_3}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_2_3}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_2_3}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_2_3}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_2_3}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_2_3}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_2_3}
3.3

Audit and Accountability (AU)

9 security requirements

3.3.1

System Auditing

Not startedReview prompts

Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. Audit logs needed (i.e., event types to be logged) to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity are specified.
  2. The content of audit records needed to support monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity is defined.
  3. Audit records are created (generated).
  4. Audit records, once created, contain the defined content.
  5. Retention requirements for audit records are defined.
  6. Audit records are retained as defined.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_3_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_3_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_3_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_1}
3.3.2

User Accountability

Not startedReview prompts

Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users so they can be held accountable for their actions.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. The content of the audit records needed to support the ability to uniquely trace users to their actions is defined.
  2. Audit records, once created, contain the defined content.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_2}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_2}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_2}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_3_2}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_3_2}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_3_2}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_2}
3.3.3

Event Review

Not startedReview prompts

Review and update logged events.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. A process for determining when to review logged events is defined.
  2. Event types being logged are reviewed in accordance with the defined review process.
  3. Event types being logged are updated based on the review.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_3}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_3}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_3}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_3}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_3}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_3}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_3_3}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_3_3}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_3_3}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_3}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_3}
3.3.4

Audit Failure Alerting

Not startedReview prompts

Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Personnel or roles to be alerted in the event of an audit logging process failure are identified.
  2. Types of audit logging process failures for which alert will be generated are defined.
  3. Identified personnel or roles are alerted in the event of an audit logging process failure.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_4}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_4}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_4}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_4}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_4}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_4}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_3_4}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_3_4}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_3_4}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_4}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_4}
3.3.5

Audit Correlation

Not startedReview prompts

Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity are defined.
  2. Defined audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes are correlated.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_5}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_5}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_5}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_5}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_5}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_5}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_3_5}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_3_5}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_3_5}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_5}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_5}
3.3.6

Reduction & Reporting

Not startedReview prompts

Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. An audit record reduction capability that supports on-demand analysis is provided.
  2. A report generation capability that supports on-demand reporting is provided.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_6}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_6}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_6}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_6}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_6}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_6}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_3_6}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_3_6}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_3_6}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_6}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_6}
3.3.7

Authoritative Time Source

Not startedReview prompts

Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source to generate time stamps for audit records.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Internal system clocks are used to generate time stamps for audit records.
  2. An authoritative source with which to compare and synchronize internal system clocks is specified.
  3. Internal system clocks used to generate time stamps for audit records are compared to and synchronized with the specified authoritative time source.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_7}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_7}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_3_7}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_3_7}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_3_7}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_3_7}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_7}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_7}
3.3.8

Audit Protection

Not startedReview prompts

Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. Audit information is protected from unauthorized access.
  2. Audit information is protected from unauthorized modification.
  3. Audit information is protected from unauthorized deletion.
  4. Audit logging tools are protected from unauthorized access.
  5. Audit logging tools are protected from unauthorized modification.
  6. Audit logging tools are protected from unauthorized deletion.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_3_8}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_3_8}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_3_8}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_3_8}
3.3.9

Audit Management

Not startedReview prompts

Limit management of audit logging functionality to a subset of privileged users.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. A subset of privileged users granted access to manage audit logging functionality is defined.
  2. Management of audit logging functionality is limited to the defined subset of privileged users.

3.4

Configuration Management (CM)

9 security requirements

3.4.1

System Baselining

Not startedReview prompts

Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. A baseline configuration is established.
  2. The baseline configuration includes hardware, software, firmware, and documentation.
  3. The baseline configuration is maintained (reviewed and updated) throughout the system development life cycle.
  4. A system inventory is established.
  5. The system inventory includes hardware, software, firmware, and documentation.
  6. The inventory is maintained (reviewed and updated) throughout the system development life cycle.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_4_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_4_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_4_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_4_1}

{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_4_1}

Security Configuration Enforcement

Not startedReview prompts

Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Security configuration settings for information technology products employed in the system are established and included in the baseline configuration.
  2. Security configuration settings for information technology products employed in the system are enforced.

System Change Management

Not startedReview prompts

Track, review, approve or disapprove, and log changes to organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Changes to the system are tracked.
  2. Changes to the system are reviewed.
  3. Changes to the system are approved or disapproved.
  4. Changes to the system are logged.

Not startedReview prompts

Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation.

Assessment objectives 1
  1. The security impact of changes to the system is analyzed prior to implementation.

3.4.5

Access Restrictions for Change

Not startedReview prompts

Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 8
  1. Physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are defined.
  2. Physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are documented.
  3. Physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are approved.
  4. Physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are enforced.
  5. Logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are defined.
  6. Logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are documented.
  7. Logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are approved.
  8. Logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are enforced.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_4_5}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_4_5}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_4_5}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_4_5}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_4_5}
3.4.6

Least Functionality

Not startedReview prompts

Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Essential system capabilities are defined based on the principle of least functionality.
  2. The system is configured to provide only the defined essential capabilities.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_4_6}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_4_6}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_4_6}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_4_6}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_4_6}
3.4.7

Nonessential Functionality

Not startedReview prompts

Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services.

Assessment objectives 15
  1. Essential programs are defined.
  2. The use of nonessential programs is defined.
  3. The use of nonessential programs is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined.
  4. Essential functions are defined.
  5. The use of nonessential functions is defined.
  6. The use of nonessential functions is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined.
  7. Essential ports are defined.
  8. The use of nonessential ports is defined.
  9. The use of nonessential ports is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined.
  10. Essential protocols are defined.
  11. The use of nonessential protocols is defined.
  12. The use of nonessential protocols is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined.
  13. Essential services are defined.
  14. The use of nonessential services is defined.
  15. The use of nonessential services is restricted, disabled, or prevented as defined.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_4_7}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_4_7}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_4_7}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_4_7}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_4_7}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_4_7}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_4_7}
3.4.8

Application Execution Policy

Not startedReview prompts

Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) policy to prevent the use of unauthorized software or deny-all, permit-by-exception (whitelisting) policy to allow the execution of authorized software.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. A policy specifying whether whitelisting or blacklisting is to be implemented is specified.
  2. The software allowed to execute under whitelisting or denied use under blacklisting is specified.
  3. Whitelisting to allow the execution of authorized software or blacklisting to prevent the use of unauthorized software is implemented as specified.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_4_8}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_4_8}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_4_8}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_4_8}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_4_8}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_4_8}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_4_8}

User-Installed Software

Not startedReview prompts

Control and monitor user-installed software.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. A policy for controlling the installation of software by users is established.
  2. Installation of software by users is controlled based on the established policy.
  3. Installation of software by users is monitored.

3.5

Identification and Authentication (IA)

11 security requirements

3.5.1

Identification

Not startedReview prompts

Identify system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. System users are identified.
  2. Processes acting on behalf of users are identified.
  3. Devices accessing the system are identified.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_5_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_5_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_5_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_5_1}

{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_5_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_5_1}
3.5.2

Authentication

Not startedReview prompts

Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. The identity of each user is authenticated or verified as a prerequisite to system access.
  2. The identity of each process acting on behalf of a user is authenticated or verified as a prerequisite to system access.
  3. The identity of each device accessing or connecting to the system is authenticated or verified as a prerequisite to system access.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_5_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_5_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_5_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_5_2}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_5_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_5_2}
3.5.3

Multifactor Authentication

Not startedReview prompts

Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Privileged accounts are identified.
  2. Multifactor authentication is implemented for local access to privileged accounts.
  3. Multifactor authentication is implemented for network access to privileged accounts.
  4. Multifactor authentication is implemented for network access to non-privileged accounts.

{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_5_3}

{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_5_3}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_5_3}

Not startedReview prompts

Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.

Assessment objectives 1
  1. Replay-resistant authentication mechanisms are implemented for network account access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.

Identifier Reuse

Prevent reuse of identifiers for a defined period.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. A period within which identifiers cannot be reused is defined.
  2. Reuse of identifiers is prevented within the defined period.

Identifier Handling

Not startedReview prompts

Disable identifiers after a defined period of inactivity.

Assessment objectives 2

  2. Identifiers are disabled after the defined period of inactivity.

Password Complexity

Not startedReview prompts

Enforce a minimum password complexity and change of characters when new passwords are created.

Assessment objectives 4
  1. Password complexity requirements are defined.
  2. Password change of character requirements are defined.
  3. Minimum password complexity requirements as defined are enforced when new passwords are created.
  4. Minimum password change of character requirements as defined are enforced when new passwords are created.

{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_5_7}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_5_7}

{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_5_7}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_5_7}

Password Reuse

Not startedReview prompts

Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. The number of generations during which a password cannot be reused is specified.
  2. Reuse of passwords is prohibited during the specified number of generations.

Temporary Passwords

Not startedReview prompts

Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change to a permanent password.

Assessment objectives 1

Not startedReview prompts

Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Passwords are cryptographically protected in storage.
  2. Passwords are cryptographically protected in transit.

Assessment objectives 1

3.6

Incident Response (IR)

3 security requirements

3.6.1

Incident Handling

Not startedReview prompts

Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.

Assessment objectives 7
  1. An operational incident-handling capability is established.
  2. The operational incident-handling capability includes preparation.
  3. The operational incident-handling capability includes detection.
  4. The operational incident-handling capability includes analysis.
  5. The operational incident-handling capability includes containment.
  6. The operational incident-handling capability includes recovery.
  7. The operational incident-handling capability includes user response activities.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_6_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_6_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_6_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_6_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_6_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_6_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_6_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_6_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_6_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_6_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_6_1}
3.6.2

Incident Reporting

Not startedReview prompts

Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. Incidents are tracked.
  2. Incidents are documented.
  3. Authorities to whom incidents are to be reported are identified.
  4. Organizational officials to whom incidents are to be reported are identified.
  5. Identified authorities are notified of incidents.
  6. Identified organizational officials are notified of incidents.
Implementation status

9 security requirements

6 security requirements

3.10.1

Limit Physical AccessCUI data

Limit physical access to organizational systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

  3. Physical access to equipment is limited to authorized individuals.

3.11

Risk Assessment (RA)

3 security requirements

3.11.1

Risk Assessments

Not startedReview prompts

Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations (including mission, functions, image, or reputation), organizational assets, and individuals, resulting from the operation of organizational systems and the associated processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. The frequency to assess risk to organizational operations, organizational assets, and individuals is defined.
  2. Risk to organizational operations, organizational assets, and individuals resulting from the operation of an organizational system that processes, stores, or transmits CUI is assessed with the defined frequency.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_11_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_11_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_11_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_11_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_11_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_11_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_11_1}
3.11.2

Vulnerability Scan

Not startedReview prompts

Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems and applications are identified.

Assessment objectives 5
  1. The frequency to scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications is defined.
  2. Vulnerability scans are performed on organizational systems with the defined frequency.
  3. Vulnerability scans are performed on applications with the defined frequency.
  4. Vulnerability scans are performed on organizational systems when new vulnerabilities are identified.
  5. Vulnerability scans are performed on applications when new vulnerabilities are identified.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_11_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_11_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_11_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_11_2}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_11_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_11_2}
3.11.3

Vulnerability Remediation

Not startedReview prompts

Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Vulnerabilities are identified.
  2. Vulnerabilities are remediated in accordance with risk assessments.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_11_3}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_11_3}

{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_11_3}
3.12

Security Assessment (CA)

4 security requirements

3.12.1

Security Control Assessment

Not startedReview prompts

Periodically assess the security controls in organizational systems to determine if the controls are effective in their application.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. The frequency of security control assessments is defined.
  2. Security controls are assessed with the defined frequency to determine if the controls are effective in their application.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_12_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_12_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_12_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_12_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_12_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_12_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_12_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_12_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_12_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_12_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_12_1}
3.12.2

Operational Plan of Action

Not startedReview prompts

Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Deficiencies and vulnerabilities to be addressed by the plan of action are identified.
  2. A plan of action is developed to correct identified deficiencies and reduce or eliminate identified vulnerabilities.
  3. The plan of action is implemented to correct identified deficiencies and reduce or eliminate identified vulnerabilities.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_12_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_12_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_12_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_12_2}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_12_2}

{CSP_PROVIDER_3_12_2}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_12_2}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_12_2}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_12_2}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_12_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_12_2}
3.12.3

Security Control Monitoring

Not startedReview prompts

Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls.

Assessment objectives 1
  1. Security controls are monitored on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of those controls.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_12_3}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_12_3}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_12_3}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_12_3}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_12_3}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.

{ESP_PROVIDER_3_12_3}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_12_3}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_12_3}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_12_3}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_12_3}
3.12.4

System Security Plan

Not startedReview prompts

Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented, and the relationships with or connections to other systems.

Assessment objectives 8
  1. A system security plan is developed.
  2. The system boundary is described and documented in the system security plan.
  3. The system environment of operation is described and documented in the system security plan.
  4. The security requirements identified and approved by the designated authority as non-applicable are identified.
  5. The method of security requirement implementation is described and documented in the system security plan.
  6. The relationship with or connection to other systems is described and documented in the system security plan.
  7. The frequency to update the system security plan is defined.
  8. System security plan is updated with the defined frequency.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_12_4}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_12_4}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_12_4}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_12_4}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_12_4}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_12_4}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_12_4}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_12_4}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_12_4}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_12_4}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_12_4}
3.13

System and Communication Protection (SC)

16 security requirements

3.13.1

Boundary Protection

Not startedReview prompts

Monitor, control, and protect communications (i.e., information transmitted or received by organizational systems) at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 8
  1. The external system boundary is defined.
  2. Key internal system boundaries are defined.
  3. Communications are monitored at the external system boundary.
  4. Communications are monitored at key internal boundaries.
  5. Communications are controlled at the external system boundary.
  6. Communications are controlled at key internal boundaries.
  7. Communications are protected at the external system boundary.
  8. Communications are protected at key internal boundaries.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_13_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_13_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_13_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_13_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_13_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_1}
3.13.2

Security Engineering

Not startedReview prompts

Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security within organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. Architectural designs that promote effective information security are identified.
  2. Software development techniques that promote effective information security are identified.
  3. Systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are identified.
  4. Identified architectural designs that promote effective information security are employed.
  5. Identified software development techniques that promote effective information security are employed.
  6. Identified systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are employed.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_2}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_13_2}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_13_2}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_13_2}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_13_2}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_13_2}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_2}
3.13.3

Role Separation

Not startedReview prompts

Separate user functionality from system management functionality.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. User functionality is identified.
  2. System management functionality is identified.
  3. User functionality is separated from system management functionality.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_3}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_3}

{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_3}

{NA_RATIONALE_3_13_3}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_3}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_3}
3.13.4

Shared Resource Control

Not startedReview prompts

Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

Assessment objectives 1
  1. Unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources is prevented.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_4}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_4}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_4}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_4}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_4}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_4}
3.13.5

Public-Access System Separation

Not startedReview prompts

Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Publicly accessible system components are identified.
  2. Subnetworks for publicly accessible system components are physically or logically separated from internal networks.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_5}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_5}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_5}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_5}

For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_5}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_5}
3.13.6

Network Communication by Exception

Not startedReview prompts

Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception).

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Network communications traffic is denied by default.
  2. Network communications traffic is allowed by exception.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_6}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_6}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_6}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_6}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_13_6}

For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_6}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_6}
3.13.7

Split Tunneling

Not startedReview prompts

Prevent remote devices from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections with organizational systems and communicating via some other connection to resources in external networks (i.e., split tunneling).

Assessment objectives 1
  1. Remote devices are prevented from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections with the system and communicating via some other connection to resources in external networks (i.e., split tunneling).
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_7}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_7}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_7}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_7}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_13_7}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_13_7}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_13_7}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_13_7}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_13_7}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_7}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_7}
3.13.8

Data in Transit

Not startedReview prompts

Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Cryptographic mechanisms intended to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI are identified.
  2. Alternative physical safeguards intended to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI are identified.
  3. Either cryptographic mechanisms or alternative physical safeguards are implemented to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_8}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_8}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_8}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_8}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_13_8}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_13_8}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_13_8}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_13_8}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_13_8}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_8}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_8}
3.13.9

Connections Termination

Not startedReview prompts

Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. A period of inactivity to terminate network connections associated with communications sessions is defined.
  2. Network connections associated with communications sessions are terminated at the end of the sessions.
  3. Network connections associated with communications sessions are terminated after the defined period of inactivity.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_9}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_9}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_13_9}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_13_9}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_13_9}

{NA_RATIONALE_3_13_9}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_13_9}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_9}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_9}
3.13.10

Key Management

Not startedReview prompts

Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Cryptographic keys are established whenever cryptography is employed.
  2. Cryptographic keys are managed whenever cryptography is employed.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_13_10}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_13_10}

Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_13_10}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_13_10}

CUI Encryption

Not startedReview prompts

Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

Assessment objectives 1
  1. FIPS-validated cryptography is employed to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

Collaborative Device Control

Not startedReview prompts

Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of devices in use to users present at the device.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Collaborative computing devices are identified.
  2. Collaborative computing devices provide indication to users of devices in use.
  3. Remote activation of collaborative computing devices is prohibited.

Control and monitor the use of mobile code.

  2. Use of mobile code is monitored.

3.14

System and Information Integrity (SI)

7 security requirements

3.14.1

Flaw Remediation

Not startedReview prompts

Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner.

Assessment objectives 6
  1. The time within which to identify system flaws is specified.
  2. System flaws are identified within the specified time frame.
  3. The time within which to report system flaws is specified.
  4. System flaws are reported within the specified time frame.
  5. The time within which to correct system flaws is specified.
  6. System flaws are corrected within the specified time frame.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_14_1}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_14_1}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_14_1}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_14_1}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_14_1}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_14_1}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_14_1}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_14_1}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_14_1}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_14_1}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_14_1}
3.14.2

Malicious Code Protection

Not startedReview prompts

Provide protection from malicious code at designated locations within organizational systems.

Assessment objectives 2
  1. Designated locations for malicious code protection are identified.
  2. Protection from malicious code at designated locations is provided.
Implementation status
{IMPLEMENTATION_STATEMENT_3_14_2}
{RESPONSIBLE_ROLE_3_14_2}
{EVIDENCE_REFERENCES_3_14_2}
{INHERITANCE_DETAILS_3_14_2}
{SCOPE_NOTES_3_14_2}
Provider inheritance and shared responsibilityDocument CSP and ESP responsibility independently.
{CSP_PROVIDER_3_14_2}
{ESP_PROVIDER_3_14_2}
{NA_RATIONALE_3_14_2}
{POAM_REFERENCE_3_14_2}
For Planned or Not Implemented / Open Gap, identify the POA&M item, remediation ticket, approved action plan, or milestone reference and target date.
Reviewer workflowNot reviewed
{REVIEWER_NOTES_3_14_2}
{AUTHOR_RESPONSE_3_14_2}
3.14.3

Security Alerts & Advisories

Not startedReview prompts

Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action in response.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. Response actions to system security alerts and advisories are identified.
  2. System security alerts and advisories are monitored.
  3. Actions in response to system security alerts and advisories are taken.

3.14.5

System & File Scanning

Not startedReview prompts

Perform periodic scans of organizational systems and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed.

Assessment objectives 3
  1. The frequency for malicious code scans is defined.
  2. Malicious code scans are performed with the defined frequency.
  3. Real-time malicious code scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed are performed.

Not startedReview prompts

Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

  3. Outbound communications traffic is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

Modernized from the supplied 62-page CMMC Level 2 System Security Plan template. Security requirement statements and assessment objectives are retained from the source document. Review all organization-specific statements, mappings, and evidence before use in an assessment or contractual submission.

Template release: v1.8.4 · Module applicability and responsibility with preserved L2G Workshop handshake and controlled return package.

Confirm action

Continue?

Document quality check

SSP Preflight

L2G Workshop handshake

Preview SSP handoff

No package loaded.

Source precedence

Lower-precedence content never replaces a current SSP or Word Review value automatically. Every control field and every status candidate requires explicit selection.

  1. Existing SSP value accepted by the author or Word Review reconciliation
  2. SSP reviewer-approved or revision-controlled value
  3. Workbook-derived reviewed overlay accepted in Workshop
  4. Advisor-confirmed Workshop value
  5. Client-confirmed statement
  6. Validated Scoper return context
  7. DocConverter extraction with provenance
  8. Generic inferred candidate

Import guardrails

    No fields selected. Existing SSP and Word Review values remain unchanged by default.
